channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High court overturns rape conviction amid inconsistencies in complainant’s evidence
item.description: The Pretoria High Court last week overturned the rape conviction of Ekurhuleni businessman Pule Mokou, which was initially handed down by the Benoni Magistrate’s Court in February 2023. Judge FMM Reid...

item.title: Temeke Council to relocate traders from Tazama pipeline for safety and modern market development
item.description: Temeke Municipal Council in Dar es Salaam is moving ahead with plans to improve safety along the Tazama pipeline by relocating traders from hazardous zones. Temeke District Commissioner, Sixtus Mapund...

item.title: US Astronauts Depart for Landmark Lunar Mission
item.description: In a major milestone for space exploration, a team of US astronauts has successfully launched on a historic journey to the Moon. The mission marks the latest chapter in NASA's ongoing efforts to explo...

item.title: Canada’s Dunstone Secures Fifth Consecutive Win, Four Teams Tied at Top of World Curling Standings
item.description: At the world men’s curling championship, Canada’s skip Matt Dunstone led his team to a fifth consecutive victory, defeating Switzerland 8-7 in a tightly contested match during Wednesday morning’s draw...

item.title: Court Hears Claims Minister Prioritised Dolphins Over Scientific Evidence in Fishing Ban
item.description: A legal battle is unfolding in Western Australia over a state government ban on certain trawling methods targeting commercial quantities of snapper and red emperor. The South African seafood company S...

item.title: PSC Acknowledges Outdated Systems as Cause of Fake Academic Certificates in Public Service
item.description: Nairobi — The Public Service Commission (PSC) has come under scrutiny after lawmakers highlighted the rise of fake academic certificates in Kenya's civil service. PSC Chairman Francis Meja admitted be...

item.title: The Role of Disinformation and Social Media in South Africa's Public Debate
item.description: The article discusses the complex landscape of disinformation and free expression in South Africa, highlighting the influence of global figures like Elon Musk on the local discourse. It contextualises...

item.title: Sam Browne: Using Poetry to Confront Toxic Masculinity and Promote Mental Health
item.description: Sam Browne, a 21-year-old performance poet from Southend, has become a viral sensation for his candid exploration of mental health and masculinity. Through his poetry, Browne addresses issues such as ...

item.title: Researchers Enhance Quantum Encryption Using the Talbot Effect
item.description: Scientists at the University of Warsaw have developed a new method for quantum key distribution (QKD) that improves both efficiency and practicality of secure communications. The team applied a 19th-c...

item.title: EFF secures MEC position in Gauteng following budget negotiations with ANC
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) have successfully secured a Member of the Executive Council (MEC) position in Gauteng after reaching an agreement with the African National Congress (ANC) during di...

item.title: Study Shows Fruit and Vegetable Placement Near Entrances Boosts Purchases
item.description: A recent study conducted by City St George's, University of London, in collaboration with the University of Southampton, University of Leeds, and Deakin University in Australia, found that placing fru...

item.title: El Nido recognized globally as a top beach destination
item.description: El Nido in Palawan has been recognized by the BBC as one of the top eight beach destinations worldwide. Known for its pristine white-sand beaches, turquoise waters, and striking limestone cliffs, El N...

item.title: Assessment of England’s 2026 World Cup Squad and Player Selection
item.description: England’s preparation for the 2026 World Cup remains uncertain, with only half of the 26-man squad appearing secure. Jordan Pickford and Harry Kane are guaranteed starters, while Declan Rice, Elliot A...

item.title: Trump comments on Iran ceasefire request; commercial LPG and jet fuel prices see rise in India
item.description: In recent developments, former US President Donald Trump claimed that Iran's 'new regime president' has requested a ceasefire. Trump did not specify clearly, as the current Iranian president, Masoud P...

item.title: NEST boosts transparency and efficiency in Tanzania's public procurement
item.description: Tanzania's government dey see big improvements since dem start using the National e-Procurement System (NEST). For the past two years, government institutions don save over Sh35 billion, combining bot...

item.title: Michael Patrick King discusses the return of The Comeback and reflections on And Just Like That
item.description: Michael Patrick King, renowned for his work on Sex and the City and its spin-off And Just Like That, has announced the return of The Comeback for a third and final season, co-created with Lisa Kudrow....

item.title: Police search for gunman after serious shooting in Bristol
item.description: A serious incident occurred in Bristol when a man in his twenties sustained potentially life-changing injuries after being shot in his car on Speedwell Road at around 13:47 BST. Avon and Somerset Poli...

item.title: Amaroo School temporarily closes classrooms due to mould
item.description: Mould was detected in the Unakita Building at Amaroo School in Canberra, prompting the temporary closure of some classrooms while remediation work is undertaken after hours. The ACT Education Director...

item.title: The Urgent Need to Address Trump’s Global Economic Impact
item.description: During his second term, Donald Trump's actions have increasingly destabilised global politics and economics. Policies such as imposing and retracting tariffs reflected inconsistent economic strategies...

item.title: Supreme Court questions Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for US-born children
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has expressed skepticism regarding former President Donald Trump's legal attempt to end birthright citizenship, a policy that grants citizenship to anyone born on Ameri...
